description: High-level document overview for query classification and routing

nodes.title: Safety Management Requirements for Defence Systems
nodes.summary: Top-level entry point for the standard, framing the overall intent: how safety is achieved, assured, and managed for defence Products, Services and/or Systems (PSS). It is used to route broad questions about what the standard is, what it covers, and how it relates to key concepts like the Safety Case and Health & Safety obligations. Use this node for high-level “what is this standard about?” and “where do I find X?” queries.

nodes.title: Introduction
nodes.summary: Sets the context for applying the standard in UK defence acquisition and through-life support, including policy drivers and how safety-related PSS are delivered. It helps answer conceptual questions about why the standard exists, who it applies to (MOD, contractors), and how it fits into acquisition governance. Route “background/rationale” and “how this fits UK MOD policy” queries here.

nodes.title: Purpose and Scope
nodes.summary: Defines what the standard is intended to achieve and the boundaries of applicability, including what types of PSS and contractual situations are in scope. It answers “is my project in scope?” and “what does the standard require at a high level?” questions. Use for routing queries about applicability, scope statements, and intended outcomes.

nodes.title: Application of Standard
nodes.summary: Explains how the standard is applied in practice, including accountability, responsibility, and the expectation that a Safety Management System is in place. It answers “who is responsible for what?” and “what must be established to apply the standard?” questions. Route queries about application rules, governance, and role allocation here.

nodes.title: Technical Information
nodes.summary: Provides the document’s technical framing and reference structure, including how normative and informative references are organized. It is used to route questions about which external standards apply and how to interpret referenced material. Use for “what standards are referenced?” and “which references are mandatory vs informative?” queries.

nodes.title: Normative References
nodes.summary: Lists references that are required to claim compliance or to correctly apply the standard. It answers “what must I follow in addition to this standard?” and “which documents are mandatory?” questions. Route compliance and dependency questions here.

nodes.title: Informative References
nodes.summary: Lists supporting guidance and related standards that inform good practice but are not necessarily mandatory for compliance. It answers “what guidance should I consult?” and “what related standards are relevant?” questions. Route “best practice” and “supporting guidance” queries here.

nodes.title: Definitions
nodes.summary: Defines terminology and rules for interpreting requirements language, including what counts as mandatory requirements and how notes should be treated. It answers “what does term X mean?” and “how do I interpret ‘shall’?” questions. Route definitional and interpretation queries here.

nodes.title: Safety Management System
nodes.summary: Specifies how a contractor’s Safety Management System (SMS) is expected to be planned, agreed, maintained, and reported, including the Safety Management Plan (SMP) as a central artifact. It answers “what must the SMS/SMP include?” and “how is it agreed and updated with MOD?” questions. Route SMS/SMP governance and planning queries here.

nodes.title: Safety Management Plan
nodes.summary: Describes the Safety Management Plan as the controlling plan for safety activities, deliverables, and governance across the contract lifecycle. It answers “what is an SMP?” and “what is it used for during tendering and delivery?” questions. Route SMP content and lifecycle questions here.

nodes.title: General Requirements
nodes.summary: Covers cross-cutting contractual and governance requirements: handling deviations, aligning with legislation/standards/policy, managing sub-contracting, defining deliverables, and maintaining the safety information set. It answers “what general obligations apply across the contract?” and “how do we manage documentation and compliance evidence?” questions. Route broad compliance-process queries here.

nodes.title: Deviation from Requirements
nodes.summary: Defines how deviations from the standard’s requirements are proposed, agreed, and documented, including conflict resolution and compliance matrices. It answers “can we deviate?” and “how do we document intended deviations?” questions. Route waiver/deviation and compliance-matrix queries here.

nodes.title: Legislation, Regulations, Standards, Policy and Approved Codes of Practice
nodes.summary: Specifies expectations for identifying, documenting, and agreeing applicable legal and policy obligations and relevant good practice. It answers “which laws/standards apply?” and “how do we agree policy/legislation applicability with MOD?” questions. Route legal/policy alignment and compliance-basis queries here.

nodes.title: Sub-Contracting
nodes.summary: Defines how safety obligations flow down to sub-contractors, including assurance, requirement identification, compliance, and audit mechanisms. It answers “what must sub-contractors do?” and “how do we assure sub-contracted safety work?” questions. Route supply-chain safety governance queries here.

nodes.title: Information Management
nodes.summary: Specifies how the safety information set is defined, maintained, kept accessible over time, and protected (including confidentiality, integrity, and availability). It answers “what is the information set?” and “how do we preserve and manage safety information through tool/technology change?” questions. Route information-set governance and retention/security queries here.

nodes.title: Documentary Deliverables
nodes.summary: Defines expectations for producing safety-related documentary deliverables (including interim versions), and agreeing format/content with MOD, considering DIDs and other standards. It answers “what documents must we deliver?” and “how do we agree deliverable format and content?” questions. Route deliverables and documentation-format queries here.

nodes.title: Roles and Responsibilities
nodes.summary: Specifies how safety roles are defined, delegated, and kept current, including competence requirements and participation in safety committees. It answers “who does what for safety?” and “what competence evidence is required?” questions. Route organizational/people/competence queries here.

nodes.title: Interfaces
nodes.summary: Defines how organisational, technical, and external interfaces are identified and managed, including recording assumptions and reconciling information between parties. It answers “how do we manage safety across interfaces?” and “what assumptions must be recorded?” questions. Route interface-management and stakeholder coordination queries here.

nodes.title: Safety Audits
nodes.summary: Specifies audit expectations, including audit reporting, independence of the Contractor Safety Auditor (CSA), independent safety audit access, and remedial action handling. It answers “what audits are required?” and “what independence/access constraints apply?” questions. Route audit/assurance and ISA/CSA queries here.

nodes.title: Safety Requirements, Hazard and Risk Analysis
nodes.summary: Core technical chapter for hazard identification, hazard tracking, safety requirements definition/management, design for safety, safety analysis, failure modes, and risk estimation/evaluation. It answers “how do we identify hazards and derive/manage safety requirements?” and “how do we estimate and evaluate risk?” questions. Route technical safety engineering and analysis queries here.

nodes.title: Hazards and Accidents
nodes.summary: Defines expectations for identifying hazards and potential accidents using systematic processes, including consideration of human factors, cyber security, systematic/random failures, and novel technologies. It answers “what must be considered during hazard identification?” and “what analysis approaches are expected?” questions. Route hazard identification and scope-of-hazard-analysis queries here.

nodes.title: Hazard Tracking
nodes.summary: Specifies how hazards are tracked and controlled over time, typically via a hazard log, including visibility of hazard control and reporting. It answers “how do we implement and maintain a hazard log?” and “what reporting is required for hazard status?” questions. Route hazard log and hazard tracking process queries here.

nodes.title: Safety Requirements
nodes.summary: Covers identification, documentation, and tracking of safety requirements, including derived safety requirements. It answers “how are safety requirements captured and traced?” and “how do we manage derived requirements?” questions. Route safety requirement definition and derivation queries here.

nodes.title: Safety Requirements Management
nodes.summary: Defines management controls for safety requirements, including bi-directional traceability, validation, evidence of fulfillment, and inclusion in the hazard log. It answers “how do we prove requirements are met?” and “what traceability is required?” questions. Route traceability/validation/evidence queries here.

nodes.title: Design for Safety
nodes.summary: Specifies how design activities must meet safety requirements, including identification, implementation, and management of mitigation strategies and allocation of derived requirements. It answers “how do we design to meet safety requirements?” and “how are mitigations selected and recorded?” questions. Route design/mitigation strategy queries here.

nodes.title: Safety Analysis
nodes.summary: Defines expectations for conducting safety analysis, ensuring adequate coverage, using results to support decisions, and documenting analysis so it remains consistent with the design. It answers “what safety analyses are required and how should they be documented?” questions. Route analysis-method and analysis-documentation queries here.

nodes.title: Failure Modes
nodes.summary: Specifies identification and management of failure modes contributing to hazards, including visibility of control status, likelihood estimation (including qualitative where needed), and recording assumptions and evidence in the information set. It answers “how do we identify failure modes and estimate likelihood?” and “what must be recorded?” questions. Route FMEA/FMECA-style and failure-mode evidence queries here.

nodes.title: Risk Estimation
nodes.summary: Defines the risk estimation process, including qualitative assessment, residual risk determination, and documentation requirements. It answers “how do we estimate risk?” and “how do we determine residual risk and record it?” questions. Route risk estimation method and documentation queries here.

nodes.title: Risk and Compliance Evaluation
nodes.summary: Specifies how risk to life and risk criteria are evaluated, how compliance is evaluated, and how results are documented and recorded. It answers “how do we judge acceptability/compliance?” and “what records are required for evaluation decisions?” questions. Route risk acceptance and compliance evaluation queries here.

nodes.title: Safety Reporting
nodes.summary: Defines required safety reporting outputs, including the Information Set Safety Summary (ISSS), Safety Case, and Safety Case Reports. It answers “what safety reports must be produced and what do they contain?” questions. Route reporting deliverables and safety case documentation queries here.

nodes.title: Information Set Safety Summary
nodes.summary: Specifies the ISSS as a curated subset of the information set to enable system integrators and operators to discharge safety responsibilities, including assumptions, limitations, and incremental delivery. It answers “what is an ISSS and what must it include?” questions. Route ISSS content and delivery cadence queries here.

nodes.title: Safety Case
nodes.summary: Defines the Safety Case as a structured argument supported by evidence drawn from the information set, addressing life-of-PSS safety and verification/validation evidence, including sub-contractor evidence. It answers “what is a safety case?” and “what evidence and structure are required?” questions. Route safety case structure, evidence, and lifecycle coverage queries here.
keywords: how to build a safety case

nodes.title: Supply and Change Management
nodes.summary: Defines how build state, change control, planning for change, safety of changes, safe update, monitoring change, and incorporating change are managed. It answers “how do we control changes without compromising safety?” and “what must be updated (design, hazard analysis, safety case, information set) after change?” questions. Route configuration/change management and safe update queries here.

nodes.title: Supporting Systems In-Service
nodes.summary: Covers in-service safety support, including management of safety-related in-service data, monitoring/reporting/data analysis, and remedial action. It answers “what must we do during in-service to maintain safety?” and “how do we use operational data to update the safety case?” questions. Route in-service monitoring, FRACAS, and remedial action queries here.

nodes.title: Service Provision
nodes.summary: Defines safety requirements for service provision, including pre-commencement safety case reporting/approval, service provision planning for normal and emergency situations, and risk management support to MOD. It answers “what must be in place before services start?” and “how is risk to life managed during service delivery?” questions. Route service operations and operational risk management queries here.

nodes.title: Safety Case
nodes.summary: Cross-cutting concept describing a structured safety argument supported by evidence, used throughout reporting, change management, and in-service support. It is central to queries about what a safety case is, what it must contain, and how it is maintained over the lifecycle. Route conceptual “definition of safety case” queries here, and procedural/requirements queries to section 10.2 and related change/in-service sections.
keywords: how to write a safety case

nodes.title: Safety Management
nodes.summary: Cross-cutting theme covering governance, planning, roles, reporting, and assurance activities that ensure safety is managed systematically. It helps route queries that are not purely technical analysis but about management controls, plans, committees, audits, and compliance processes. Use to route to sections 4–8 and general requirements in section 5.

nodes.title: Safety Engineering
nodes.summary: Cross-cutting theme for technical safety activities: hazard analysis, requirements derivation, design for safety, and safety analysis. It routes technical questions about methods, evidence, and engineering practices to section 9 and safety case evidence requirements in section 10.2. Use for “how do we engineer safety?” queries.

nodes.title: Safety In-Service
nodes.summary: Cross-cutting theme for maintaining and assuring safety during operation and support, including monitoring, reporting, data analysis, and remedial action. It routes queries about operational feedback, incident reporting, and updating safety arguments to section 12 and service provision section 13. Use for “in-service safety obligations” queries.

nodes.title: ALARP principle
nodes.summary: Defines the ALARP concept used for judging risk acceptability and demonstrating that risk has been reduced as low as reasonably practicable. It routes queries about risk acceptance rationale and risk-to-life decision-making to risk evaluation (9.9) and service risk management (13.3). Use for “what is ALARP?” and “how is ALARP applied?” queries.

nodes.title: Defence Standard 00-055
nodes.summary: Frequently cited external standard governing safety of programmable elements (PE) in defence systems, referenced across hazard analysis, failure considerations, and compliance evidence. It is used when queries involve software/programmable logic safety obligations, systematic failures, or PE-specific requirements. Route “what does 00-055 require?” and “how does 00-056 relate to 00-055?” queries here.

nodes.title: ASEMS DE&S Acquisition Safety and Environmental Management System
nodes.summary: Frequently cited MOD acquisition safety and environmental management framework used as supporting guidance for safety management planning and governance. It is relevant to queries about how MOD expects safety to be managed within acquisition processes and how contractor plans align with MOD systems. Route “ASEMS guidance” and “alignment with DE&S processes” queries here.
keywords: how to align with MOD processes

nodes.title: POSMS
nodes.summary: Frequently cited MOD safety management system reference used to support project-oriented safety management practices and initiation procedures. It is relevant to queries about MOD safety management processes, initiation, and how contractor safety management integrates with MOD expectations. Route “POSMS process” and “project safety management system” queries here.

nodes.title: Def Stan 05-057 Configuration Management of Defence Materiel
nodes.summary: Frequently cited configuration management standard used to support information set consistency and change/build state control. It is relevant to queries about configuration management expectations, build state definition, and maintaining consistency of safety evidence across changes. Route configuration management dependency queries here.

nodes.title: Knowledge in Defence website
nodes.summary: Frequently cited MOD knowledge portal referenced for accessing defence standards and safety-related competence resources. It is relevant to queries about where to obtain standards, competence frameworks, and supporting materials. Route “where do I find X?” and “how to access MOD resources” queries here.
